betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Reactor Builder§f:
The Solid Fuel Fission Controller (nuclearcraft:solid_fission_controller) is the central block of a Solid Fission Reactor multiblock. This questline teaches you how to assemble the full reactor from individual components.

§6Controller Crafting Recipe (CraftingRecipeHandler.java:275)§f:
Pattern "PTP / HFH / PTP" in a 3×3 grid. Letters: P = plateAdvanced, T = ingotTough, H = ingotHardCarbon, F = steelFrame. Yields 1 Solid Fission Controller.

§6Reactor Construction Sequence§f:
A Solid Fission Reactor is built bottom-up:

§6Step 1: Place the Controller§f:
The Controller is placed at any position in the bottom-center of the reactor. It defines the multiblock's footprint and provides the GUI for fuel loading and status monitoring.

§6Step 2: Build the Casing Shell§f:
Surround the controller with Fission Reactor Casing blocks. The casing forms a hollow cube. Minimum size is 3×3×3 (interior 1×1×1); practical sizes range from 5×5×5 to 19×19×19.

§6Step 3: Add the Interior§f:
Fill the interior with:
- §bFission Fuel Cells§f (the fuel-containing tiles)
- §bHeat Sinks§f (passive coolers)
- §bModerators§f (graphite, beryllium, or heavy water)
- §bReflectors§f (beryllium or lead, on wall faces)
- §bIrradiators§f (item processing)
- §bNeutron Sources§f (startup triggers)
- §bCell Ports§f (fluid-based heat extraction)
- §bVents§f (heat venting to air)

§6Step 4: Form the Reactor§f:
Right-click the Controller with a Reactor Simulator (NC:reactor_simulator) or a §bReactor Builder's Tool§f to validate the structure. If valid, the multiblock forms and the controller becomes the access hatch.

§6Components in the Controller Recipe (TaskRetrieval)§f:
The graph shows 4 items that go into the controller:
- plateAdvanced (nuclearcraft:part:1) — high-tier structural plate
- ingotTough (nuclearcraft:alloy:1) — Tough Alloy
- ingotHardCarbon (nuclearcraft:alloy:2) — Hard Carbon Alloy
- steelFrame (nuclearcraft:part:11) — basic steel chassis

§6Fuel Loading§f:
Once the reactor is formed, place LEU-235, HEU-235, MOX, or other fuel pellets/rods into the fuel column slots. The reactor will start producing heat and RF once neutron flux exceeds criticality.

§6Tuning the Reactor§f:
Use the Reactor Simulator (NC item) to preview stats before building. Adjust:
- Reflector placement for higher flux
- Moderator placement for higher efficiency
- Heat Sink count for thermal stability
- Coolant fluid choice for higher heat removal rate

§6Prerequisites§f:
Requires q30043 (Solid Fission Reactor) — the parent quest for this component.

§eTip§f: Start with a 5×5×5 large reactor and 4-8 LEU-235 pellets. This gives a manageable heat load while teaching the design principles.

§eTip§f: Place a §bReactor Info Panel§f adjacent to the controller. It displays live stats (heat, flux, efficiency) without opening the GUI.

§cWarning§f: A poorly-designed reactor can melt down within seconds. Always simulate first, use a low-enrichment fuel for initial testing, and have a manual shutdown lever within reach.
